title: aquilegia vulgaris f. plena
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris f. plena, also known as the double Columbine or granny's bonnet, is a flowering plant species in the buttercup family that is native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its double-petaled flowers in shades of red, pink, and purple. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Aquilegia vulgaris f. plena include its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to relieve congestion and coughs, as well as its antimicrobial properties which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems.
